name: wal_crc32_mismatch
raw_log:
2026/05/24 07:43:17 ERROR queue: WAL replay failed for queue "orders": wal: CRC mismatch
goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/wal.ReadAll(...)
        /app/internal/wal/wal.go:298 +0x1c7
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.NewQueue(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:80 +0x93
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.(*Manager).Get(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:197 +0x112
main.main()
        /app/cmd/broker/main.go:70 +0x2a4
exit status 1
correct_root_cause: ReadAll encountered a CRC32 mismatch mid-file during WAL replay in NewQueue, indicating a torn write left a corrupt record at the boundary; the broker cannot enqueue the recovered messages and exits.
correct_reasoning: A CRC mismatch during startup WAL replay means the broker cannot reconstruct queue state; messages after the torn-write boundary are permanently unrecoverable without operator intervention to truncate or restore from backup, so the severity is CRITICAL.
recovery_action: Identify the corrupt WAL file under COURIER_WAL_DIR (e.g. data/wal/orders.wal) by inspecting the log for the queue name.
Truncate the WAL at the last valid record boundary using a hex editor or a custom tool that re-reads records up to the first CRC mismatch and writes them to a new file.
Restore the truncated WAL file in place and restart the broker; monitor logs to confirm ReadAll completes without ErrCRCMismatch.

name: wal_dir_missing
raw_log:
2026/05/24 14:23:09 ERROR queue: failed to open WAL: open data/wal/payments.wal: no such file or directory
goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/wal.Open(...)
        /app/internal/wal/wal.go:205 +0x61
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.NewQueue(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:67 +0x77
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.(*Manager).Get(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:197 +0x112
main.main()
        /app/cmd/broker/main.go:70 +0x2a4
exit status 1
correct_root_cause: The WAL directory specified by COURIER_WAL_DIR does not exist and was not pre-created; wal.Open calls os.OpenFile which fails with 'no such file or directory', preventing NewQueue from initialising and causing the broker to exit.
correct_reasoning: Without a writable WAL directory the broker cannot persist any messages and cannot start at all; this is a configuration/deployment error that requires operator action to create the directory, so it is CRITICAL.
recovery_action: Create the WAL directory referenced by COURIER_WAL_DIR (default: data/wal) with appropriate permissions before starting the broker.
Verify the directory is writable by the broker process user and that any volume mounts (in Docker/Kubernetes) are correctly configured.
Restart the broker and confirm the log shows 'broker: configuration loaded' without any WAL open errors.

name: dispatch_timeout_retry
raw_log:
2026/05/24 09:51:33 ERROR retry: failed to nack timed-out message msg_id=a3f8c1d2-04bb-4e7a-b91c-38d7e6f02a11 consumer=consumer-7 error=<nil>
2026/05/24 09:51:33 INFO retry: timed out messages count=1 total_in_flight=4
goroutine 12 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/retry.(*Scanner).scan(...)
        /app/internal/retry/scanner.go:123 +0x88
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/retry.(*Scanner).loop(...)
        /app/internal/retry/scanner.go:110 +0x54
correct_root_cause: The retry Scanner detected that message a3f8c1d2 remained in-flight longer than COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT; it called ackmgr.Nack which re-enqueued the message via Queue.Enqueue with an incremented RetryCount, so delivery will be reattempted.
correct_reasoning: Dispatch timeouts are a normal transient failure path explicitly handled by the retry scanner; the message is re-enqueued with incremented RetryCount and delivery continues automatically, so the severity is MEDIUM.
recovery_action: Check consumer health and gRPC stream latency for consumer-7 to determine why the ACK was not received within COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT.
Consider increasing COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT if consumer processing is legitimately slow, or reducing payload size to speed up processing.
Monitor courier_retry_timeouts_total metric to confirm the retry rate returns to baseline after the consumer issue is resolved.

name: max_retries_exhausted_dlq
raw_log:
2026/05/24 18:07:41 ERROR ackmgr: routing dead message to DLQ msg_id=b7e2d9a0-c341-4f83-ae12-91c04f7b3318 queue=orders dlq=orders.dlq retry_count=3
goroutine 12 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/ackmgr.(*Manager).Nack(...)
        /app/internal/ackmgr/ackmgr.go:127 +0xb9
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/retry.(*Scanner).scan(...)
        /app/internal/retry/scanner.go:123 +0x88
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/retry.(*Scanner).loop(...)
        /app/internal/retry/scanner.go:110 +0x54
correct_root_cause: Message b7e2d9a0 on the 'orders' queue had its RetryCount reach COURIER_MAX_RETRIES (3); Manager.Nack called msg.IsDead which returned true, so the message status was set to StatusDead and it was enqueued onto 'orders.dlq' via queue.Manager.Get.
correct_reasoning: A message reaching the DLQ means it has failed delivery COURIER_MAX_RETRIES times and will not be retried automatically; operator investigation is required to understand the root cause and replay or discard the dead-lettered message, so the severity is HIGH.
recovery_action: Inspect the orders.dlq WAL file and identify the payload of message b7e2d9a0 to understand why it failed consistently.
Diagnose the consumer-side processing error (e.g. schema mismatch, downstream service unavailability) that caused repeated NACKs.
After fixing the consumer, replay the message by re-publishing it to the 'orders' queue and drain 'orders.dlq' once the root cause is resolved.

name: consumer_stream_closed_mid_delivery
raw_log:
2026/05/24 03:47:52 WARN dispatcher: consumer stream closed during send msg_id=c9a1f3b4-7d56-4891-b023-5e8f2d1c9a34 consumer=consumer-2 queue=notifications
goroutine 19 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/dispatcher.(*Dispatcher).dispatch(...)
        /app/internal/dispatcher/dispatcher.go:122 +0xd3
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/dispatcher.(*Dispatcher).EnsureRunning.func1(...)
        /app/internal/dispatcher/dispatcher.go:68 +0x41
correct_root_cause: The consumer gRPC stream for consumer-2 closed between Register being called and the message being received; the message is tracked in Manager.inFlight but never consumed, and will be re-enqueued automatically when Scanner.scan detects it has exceeded COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT.
correct_reasoning: A mid-delivery stream disconnect is a transient network event; the AckManager will timeout the in-flight entry and Scanner.Nack will re-enqueue the message for delivery to another consumer, so no data is permanently lost and the severity is LOW.
recovery_action: Confirm the message c9a1f3b4 is re-enqueued by checking courier_retry_timeouts_total and courier_queue_depth_total for the 'notifications' queue after COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT elapses.
Investigate why consumer-2's gRPC stream was closed (network interruption, client-side timeout, or process crash).
Ensure consumers implement reconnect-with-backoff logic to reduce the frequency of mid-delivery disconnects.

name: wal_compaction_rename_failed
raw_log:
2026/05/24 11:58:29 ERROR queue: WAL compaction rename failed src=data/wal/orders.wal.tmp dst=data/wal/orders.wal error=rename data/wal/orders.wal.tmp data/wal/orders.wal: cross-device link
goroutine 7 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/wal.Compact(...)
        /app/internal/wal/wal.go:340 +0x1a2
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.(*Queue).WriteTombstone(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:144 +0x7c
correct_root_cause: wal.Compact wrote the compacted records to 'orders.wal.tmp' (path + ".tmp") but the final os.Rename call failed with a cross-device link error, leaving the original WAL uncompacted; the broker continues running with the uncompacted file.
correct_reasoning: A failed compaction rename leaves the original WAL intact and the broker continues processing normally; the only consequence is wasted disk space from dead entries, and compaction will be reattempted; this is MEDIUM severity.
recovery_action: Remove the leftover 'orders.wal.tmp' file to avoid stale data accumulation on disk.
Ensure COURIER_WAL_DIR is on the same filesystem as any temp directories to prevent cross-device rename failures; avoid bind-mounting WAL dirs across different block devices.
Monitor disk usage for the WAL directory and manually trigger compaction or restart the broker to reattempt compaction once the filesystem issue is fixed.

name: wal_partial_write_last_record
raw_log:
2026/05/24 22:14:53 ERROR queue: WAL replay stopped at torn write boundary queue=payments recovered=47 lost=1 error=wal: CRC mismatch
goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/wal.ReadAll(...)
        /app/internal/wal/wal.go:299 +0x1c7
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.NewQueue(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:80 +0x93
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.(*Manager).Get(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:197 +0x112
main.main()
        /app/cmd/broker/main.go:70 +0x2a4
correct_root_cause: A process crash occurred during WAL.Append's file.Sync for the last record in the 'payments' WAL; ReadAll stops at the CRC mismatch boundary and returns the prior 47 records, but the final record's payload is irrecoverably lost.
correct_reasoning: Although prior records are recovered and the broker can start, the last message in the WAL is permanently lost due to the partial fsync; producers will not automatically re-send it without external intervention, making this HIGH severity.
recovery_action: Confirm that exactly one message is missing by correlating the producer's send log with the recovered record count from the WAL replay log.
Notify the upstream producer system to re-publish the lost message; the broker itself has no mechanism to recover data past the CRC mismatch boundary.
Consider enabling O_SYNC or using WAL pre-allocation strategies to reduce the torn-write window on crash, and ensure the host uses a journaling filesystem.

name: graceful_shutdown_drain_timeout
raw_log:
2026/05/24 16:39:07 INFO broker: shutdown signal received
2026/05/24 16:39:22 WARN broker: graceful shutdown timed out, forcing stop
goroutine 1 [running]:
main.main()
        /app/cmd/broker/main.go:143 +0x3b1
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.(*Manager).Close(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:225 +0x8f
main.main()
        /app/cmd/broker/main.go:147 +0x3c9
correct_root_cause: After receiving SIGTERM, gs.GracefulStop() did not complete within COURIER_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT (15s) because in-flight RPCs were still active; main called gs.Stop() to force-terminate, but qm.Close() still flushed WAL files successfully so data is recoverable on next start.
correct_reasoning: A forced shutdown means some in-flight gRPC calls were interrupted, but WAL durability ensures messages can be replayed on the next broker start; the retry scanner will re-enqueue any timed-out in-flight messages, so this is MEDIUM severity.
recovery_action: Increase COURIER_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT to give in-flight RPCs more time to complete before the force-stop path is triggered.
On next broker start, verify WAL replay succeeds for all queues and that in-flight messages from before the shutdown are re-enqueued by checking queue depth metrics.
Investigate why RPCs were taking longer than COURIER_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT (e.g. slow consumers, large payloads) and tune COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT accordingly.

name: wal_replay_channel_overflow
raw_log:
2026/05/24 08:22:47 WARN queue: channel full during WAL replay, dropping message msg_id=d4b7e2f1-9c83-41a7-b56e-72f3d8c01a59 queue=events
goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.NewQueue(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:92 +0xa1
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/queue.(*Manager).Get(...)
        /app/internal/queue/queue.go:197 +0x112
main.main()
        /app/cmd/broker/main.go:70 +0x2a4
correct_root_cause: During WAL replay in NewQueue, the number of pending StatusPending records exceeded channelCap (1024); the non-blocking select in the replay loop hit the default branch and silently dropped overflow messages, causing permanent silent data loss.
correct_reasoning: Messages dropped via the default select branch during replay are permanently lost since no error is returned and no retry occurs; the WAL still contains those records but they will never be delivered, making this a silent data-loss bug of HIGH severity.
recovery_action: Identify which message IDs were dropped by correlating WAL record IDs with those actually loaded into the channel (channelCap=1024 is the cutoff).
Re-publish the dropped messages from the upstream producer or a backup store before the WAL is compacted and the records are removed.
Increase channelCap in queue/queue.go to accommodate the maximum expected pending message count, or implement backpressure in NewQueue to return an error instead of silently dropping.

name: ackmgr_unknown_message_id
raw_log:
2026/05/24 13:06:38 WARN ackmgr: ack for unknown message id msg_id=e5c3a8b0-2f74-4d19-c87a-46e9f1d02b73 error="ackmgr: message e5c3a8b0-2f74-4d19-c87a-46e9f1d02b73 not in flight"
goroutine 23 [running]:
github.com/Wali05/courier/internal/ackmgr.(*Manager).Ack(...)
        /app/internal/ackmgr/ackmgr.go:77 +0x6e
correct_root_cause: A consumer sent a duplicate ACK for message e5c3a8b0 after COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT had already elapsed; the retry scanner had previously called Nack which deleted the entry from Manager.inFlight, so the late ACK finds no entry and Manager.Ack returns 'not in flight'; the warning is logged and the ACK is ignored.
correct_reasoning: A duplicate ACK for an already-removed in-flight entry is a benign race between the consumer's delayed ACK and the scanner's timeout NACK; the message has already been re-enqueued and will be delivered again, so no data is lost and the severity is LOW.
recovery_action: Verify that the message e5c3a8b0 was successfully re-enqueued by the retry scanner and subsequently delivered to a consumer by checking courier_messages_retried_total.
Investigate why the consumer ACK was delayed beyond COURIER_DISPATCH_TIMEOUT; consider increasing the timeout or optimising consumer processing latency.
Ensure consumers handle idempotent delivery gracefully since the message will be delivered at least once more after the scanner re-enqueues it.
